channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Ukraine War Duration Exceeds World War I
item.description: The conflict in Ukraine has now lasted longer than World War I, marking a grim milestone as fighting continues with no clear end in sight. The war has entered its fourth year with ongoing casualties and infrastructure destruction.

item.title: 🇹🇭⛪ Thai Court Sentences Two Uyghurs to Death for 2015 Shrine Bombing
item.description: A Thai court has sentenced two Uyghur men to death for the 2015 Erawan Shrine bombing in Bangkok that killed 20 people. The attack had targeted tourists in the popular Hindu shrine.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷💥 US Bases in Middle East Face Second Round of Iranian Retaliatory Attacks
item.description: US military bases across the Middle East are experiencing a second wave of Iranian retaliatory strikes following earlier American attacks. The escalation threatens to further destabilize the already fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇺🇦🎯 Ukraine Launches Long-Range Strikes on Russian Military and Energy Sites
item.description: Ukrainian forces have carried out long-range strikes targeting military installations and energy infrastructure inside Russia. The attacks come as the war surpasses the duration of World War I.

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 Two Babies Die of Ebola in Congo Orphanage as Outbreak Spreads
item.description: Two infants have died of Ebola in a Congo orphanage, highlighting the particular risk children face in the ongoing outbreak. WHO reports testing has been stalled in three labs due to supply shortages.

item.title: ⛪🚢 Pope Leo Heads to Canary Islands to Highlight Migrant Crisis
item.description: Pope Leo traveled to the Canary Islands to meet migrants who have braved dangerous Atlantic crossings, urging more humane treatment of immigrants as the Mediterranean route faces record arrivals.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Oil Prices Jump as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz After US Strikes
item.description: Oil prices climbed more than $2 per barrel after Iran declared closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway following US military strikes, though gains were later pared as traders assessed actual supply disruption impact.

item.title: 🇰🇼 Kuwait Reopens Airspace After Temporary Closure Following Iranian Attacks
item.description: Kuwait has reopened its airspace after a temporary closure due to Iranian attacks on the region, as Gulf states face mounting security concerns from the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Gulf Countries Say Iran Aggression Closes Door to Dialogue
item.description: Gulf Arab states have condemned Irans recent attacks, stating that Tehrans aggression has effectively ended any prospect for diplomatic engagement in the near term.

item.title: ⚔️ US Military Confirms No Warships Struck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military has confirmed that none of its naval vessels were hit in the Strait of Hormuz, amid Iranian claims of successful strikes on American assets.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand Sentences Chinese Uyghurs to Death in 2015 Shrine Bombing Case
item.description: A Thai court has sentenced two Chinese Uyghurs to death for their involvement in the 2015 Bangkok shrine bombing that killed 20 people, in a case with geopolitical implications.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump to Meet Defense Firms Over Weapons Shortage Concerns
item.description: The White House is convening defense industry leaders to discuss ramping up production amid growing concerns over missile supply and depleted stockpiles from ongoing conflicts.

item.title: ⚽ FIFA World Cup 2026 Kicks Off Amid Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup begins with Iran war and visa restrictions casting a shadow over football's biggest tournament. Politics looms large as the US co-host prepares to perform under global scrutiny.

item.title: 🏛️ G7 Summit Opens with Tight Security on Swiss-French Border
item.description: French and Swiss authorities imposed pandemic-like border restrictions as G7 leaders including President Trump gathered for the summit. Security measures are intense amid concerns about potential violent protests.

item.title: 🌍 UK Venture Plans Europe's Largest Direct Air Carbon Capture Plant
item.description: A new UK company called UnionDAC aims to become a leader in removing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere, planning Europe's largest direct air capture facility as climate technology investment accelerates.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine Strikes Crimea in Large-Scale Attack Across Peninsula
item.description: Explosions reported across Russian-occupied Crimea as Ukraine launches apparent large-scale attack on the peninsula, targeting military infrastructure.

item.title: 🇮🇷💧 US Strikes Hit Iranian Drinking Water Facility Near Hormuz
item.description: US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz reportedly hit an Iranian drinking water facility, raising concerns about civilian infrastructure targeting.

item.title: 🇯🇴⚔️ IRGC Claims Targeting US Fighter Jet Base and Military Facilities in Jordan
item.description: Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps says it targeted US military facilities including a fighter jet base in Jordan in retaliatory strikes.

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Trump Says Over 100 Million Barrels of Oil Passed Through Hormuz
item.description: President Trump claims significant oil volumes continue through the strategic Strait of Hormuz despite Iranian threats to close the vital waterway.

item.title: 🇪🇺 European Central Bank Set for Insurance Rate Hike as Iran War Fans Inflation
item.description: The European Central Bank is all but certain to raise interest rates on Thursday in the hope of nipping higher inflation in the bud before a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war spreads more broadly across the euro zone economy.

item.title: 🛢️ US Becomes Worlds Top Oil Exporter, Upending Decades-Old Global Energy Order
item.description: The United States has become the worlds largest oil exporter, overtaking Saudi Arabia and Russia in a historic shift that tightens American companies grip on energy markets as Washingtons war with Iran reshapes global energy trade.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support for Beirut Government
item.description: Saudi Arabia has lifted a roughly five-year ban on imports from Lebanon in a show of support for Beiruts embattled government, offering relief to businesses battered by years of conflict between Hezbollah and Israel.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Campaign Continues
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

item.title: ⚔️ US and Iran Trade Air Attacks for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged air attacks for a second straight day, with President Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The renewed fighting has cast fresh doubt over prospects for ending the conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran and US exchange fire as hostilities escalate across Gulf
item.description: Iran retaliated against US strikes with attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, while the US military announced it completed additional strikes on multiple Iranian targets. Bahrain activated missile warning sirens and Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace.
